task.title: Migrate public coordination API to session queue scoped leases
task.summary: Replace public claim vocabulary with artifact-status-first session queue coordination while preserving transitional compatibility for existing claim/lease callers.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki public coordination language/API expose artifact availability, in-use, waiting, conflict, and stale status from session queue state; sessions are holders/waiters behind artifact status, and /wiki-resume allocates safe parallel work from that state.
acceptance: Domain/session queue types model artifact statuses for tasks, paths, builds, validation refs, generated state, and publication refs with holders, waiters, heartbeat/TTL, handoff metadata, and isolation context.
Public API/tool/UI/graph/prompt language uses artifact status terminology (available, in-use, waiting, conflict, stale, holder, waiter) instead of claim/lease as primary concepts.
Legacy codewiki_claim and any lease/claim naming remains only as compatibility/internal implementation detail with deprecation/migration notes if needed.
New or renamed application tool/API surface exposes artifact-status operations: inspect status, mark/use artifact for read/write work, wait, heartbeat, release, and list holders/waiters.
Storage remains .codewiki/session/queue.json unless migration planning explicitly changes it.
Tests cover artifact status create/list/release/heartbeat/wait behavior and legacy alias compatibility.

/wiki-resume without an explicit task reads fresh CodeWiki state plus session queue artifact statuses and selects the next unblocked atomic task whose relevant artifacts are available or read-compatible.
/wiki-resume TASK-### honors an explicit non-closed task request even when an umbrella task is active, unless task policy or artifact conflict provides a real block with a clear explanation.

Resume output includes selected task, artifact statuses, conflicting artifacts and holders/waiters, temporary session usage record, and helper-safe next steps so one tab/session can start parallel work with a single /wiki-resume invocation.
non_goals: Do not break existing users abruptly by deleting codewiki_claim before compatibility alias and migration notes exist.
Do not treat session queue artifact status as durable roadmap truth.
Do not use broad umbrella tasks such as TASK-077 as default implementation work when safe child/atomic tasks are available.
Do not make claim or lease primary project lexicon terms.
verification: targeted legacy claim/lease alias compatibility smoke
targeted /wiki-resume parallel allocation smoke with active umbrella focus and artifact conflict fixtures
stale-term scan for public claim/lease vocabulary with allowed compatibility/internal exceptions only

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Implementation validation must not reuse builder thought context and must cite checked content proof.

next_loop.reason: The next gateway must independently validate implementation evidence and cite checked content proof.

summary: Implemented TASK-086 first slice: /wiki-resume now uses artifact-status/session queue state to skip umbrella tasks, honor explicit child tasks, avoid artifact conflicts, and mark selected artifacts in-use by the session.

evidence: Added resume-scheduler smoke covering umbrella skip, explicit child honoring, conflict skip to next task, and explicit conflict block.
Updated package smoke expectation from old active-loop barrier to explicit-task artifact-status start.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Added artifact status records to change-claim/session queue state without changing storage path.
Updated /wiki-resume scheduler to use artifact status rather than persisted active umbrella focus as the main allocator.
Added automatic session queue usage marking for selected task artifacts after resume starts work.
notes: This is a first slice of TASK-086 focused on /wiki-resume scheduler behavior. Legacy codewiki_claim public alias and broader claim/lease terminology migration remain open under TASK-086 acceptance.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Added resume-scheduler smoke covering umbrella skip, explicit child honoring, conflict skip to next task, and explicit conflict block.
Updated package smoke expectation from old active-loop barrier to explicit-task artifact-status start.
code_change_evidence: Added artifact status records to change-claim/session queue state without changing storage path.
Updated /wiki-resume scheduler to use artifact status rather than persisted active umbrella focus as the main allocator.
Added automatic session queue usage marking for selected task artifacts after resume starts work.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: /wiki-resume without an explicit task reads fresh roadmap/session queue artifact status and selects the next unblocked atomic task.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: src/adapters/pi/commands/resume.ts resolves candidates from active link, persisted focus, and roadmap order; skips umbrella tasks and conflicting artifact statuses; tests/smoke/resume-scheduler.test.mjs asserts persisted TASK-077 umbrella is skipped for TASK-083.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: /wiki-resume TASK-### honors explicit child tasks unless real artifact conflict exists.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: resolveImplementationTask now checks requested task artifact statuses instead of blocking on active loop status; resume scheduler smoke asserts explicit TASK-083 is honored and explicit TASK-083 blocks only when another session holds conflicting artifacts.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Resume output includes artifact status and temporary session usage record.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: runResumeCommand now records selected artifacts through session queue via mutateChangeClaims, adds artifact preflight to injected prompt evidence, and notifies that artifacts are marked in-use by this session.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Session queue state can expose artifact statuses with holders/waiters/conflicts.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: src/domain/shared/types.ts adds ArtifactStatusRecord/Holder; src/application/claims.ts builds artifact_statuses from active claims/waiters/conflicts and offers scoped availability checks.

closure_brief.user_intent: Fix /wiki-resume first so future helper sessions can parallelize from CodeWiki state instead of manual prompts.
implemented_changes: Added artifact-status records derived from session queue state.
Changed /wiki-resume to select artifact-available atomic tasks and skip umbrella coordination tasks.
Changed explicit /wiki-resume TASK-### to honor requested tasks unless artifact status conflicts.
Recorded selected task artifacts as in-use by the session queue.
non_goals_preserved: Did not remove codewiki_claim legacy alias in this slice.
Did not move resume prompt templates to skill assets; TASK-083 owns that.
Did not treat session queue state as durable roadmap truth.
remaining_risks: Public claim/lease terminology and new artifact-status tool/API migration remain to finish TASK-086.

risks: Task remains in_progress because compatibility API rename/stale public vocabulary migration is not complete.

restore.note: Restored history is reference material until promoted into active knowledge or roadmap truth.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

commit.title: chore(codewiki): record TASK-086 Migrate public coordination API to session queue scoped leases implementation evidence
commit.body:
Implemented TASK-086 first slice: /wiki-resume now uses artifact-status/session queue state to skip umbrella tasks, honor explicit child tasks, avoid artifact conflicts, and mark selected artifacts in-use by the session.

Checks: npm run typecheck, node ./tests/smoke/resume-scheduler.test.mjs, npm run test:smoke, npm run check:architecture, npm test
Validation: no durable validation refs recorded.
Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
